title: EMQX
description: Massively scalable open-source MQTT broker for IoT, connecting millions of devices with a built-in dashboard, rules engine and data bridges.
installation: EMQX is self-contained: it stores cluster state, sessions, retained messages and config in its built-in Mnesia/RLOG database persisted to the emqx-data volume at /opt/emqx/data, so no external add-on is provisioned. The HTTP management dashboard and REST API listen on port 18083; the MQTT listeners (1883 TCP, 8883 TLS, 8083 WS, 8084 WSS) are exposed by the image but are not the HTTP container port and need a separate Service/Ingress if you want external MQTT access. On first run log in to the dashboard with the default account admin / public and change the password immediately (EMQX_DASHBOARD__DEFAULT_PASSWORD is set to a placeholder here that you should rotate, or just change it in the UI). EMQX_NODE__COOKIE is the Erlang distribution secret shared by cluster members and MUST be rotated from the template default before production. Single-node runs on the open-source edition; from v5.9 clustering requires a license key from EMQX, and optional auth/data integrations (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, Kafka, MongoDB, etc.) are configured later in the dashboard, not at boot.
